word: H1058 - בָּכָה - bâkâh - baw-kaw' - [idiom] at all

sections.paragraph:
Original: בּכה

Transliteration: bâkâh

Phonetic: baw-kaw'

BDB Definition:

  1. to weep, bewail, cry, shed tears
    1. (Qal)
      1. to weep (in grief, humiliation, or joy)
      2. to weep bitterly (with cognate acc.)
      3. to weep upon (embrace and weep)
      4. to bewail
    2. (Piel) participle
      1. lamenting
      2. bewailing

Origin: a primitive root

TWOT entry: 243

Part(s) of speech: Verb
